page: Amazon Web Services r8id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, this x86_64 architecture configuration provides 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system features a substantial L3 cache of 480 MB and is equipped with 512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed physical disk access directly attached to the Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The hardware configuration lacks dedicated GPU accelerators, meaning all parallel processing and vector calculations rely entirely on the CPU's instruction sets, which include Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in several compute and memory categories. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth tests show strong performance for read-only operations, reaching 5121.18 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 191.69 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth remains in the average tier at 78.73 GB/sec. Additionally, the CPU's AMX capabilities enable top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models, though text generation speeds for larger 70B models drop to an average of 3.34 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r8id.16xlarge offers a dense allocation of DDR5 memory and local NVMe SSD storage, reducing the need for external network-attached storage for data-intensive operations. This makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads, such as large-scale relational databases, Redis in-memory caching, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 512 GB of DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed via the Nitro hypervisor. The instance delivers top-tier CPU performance, as evidenced by its high Geekbench and Passmark scores, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of up to 191.69 GB/sec for large blocks. While it lacks dedicated GPUs, its CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, which drive top-tier prompt processing speeds for LLM inference. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive databases, Redis deployments, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform combining high-speed DDR5 memory, local NVMe storage, and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz.
512 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier CPU performance in Geekbench and Passmark benchmarks.
Strong memory read bandwidth and high-throughput static web serving capabilities.
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and CPU-based LLM inference.
